strengths: Sustained an exceptionally high aggregate Win Rate of 92.3% over a statistically significant sample of 562 trades.
The 'opening 15 minutes' regime filter successfully increased per-trade profitability, boosting expectancy to +6.23 bps from +5.95 bps in the parent spec (iter002_obi1_and_microprice_consensus).
Demonstrates high profitability with a strong aggregate expectancy of +6.23 bps per trade.
weaknesses: The regime filter drastically reduced the number of trades by over 87% (from 4381 in the parent to 562), which may limit its practical deployment.
Performance shows some symbol and day-to-day variance, with daily Win Rates ranging from 86.3% to 96.2%, a spread of 9.9 percentage points, indicating mixed consistency.
The Win Rate remains anomalously high at 92.3%, raising concerns about potential backtest idealizations (e.g., zero latency, no fees) that might not hold in a live environment.
recommended_direction_reasoning: The spec confirms a strong candidate edge with a WR of 92.3% and positive expectancy of +6.23 bps over 562 trades. The time-of-day filter successfully increased profitability. The next logical step is to tighten the underlying signal thresholds (e.g., obi_1 > 0.6, microprice_dev_bps > 1.2) to see if an even higher-conviction subset of this profitable regime can be isolated, potentially increasing both WR and expectancy further.
